name: Hearthglen
region: Northern Lordaeron, fortified town and military staging ground on the southern line of the plague response.

sensoryProfile.sight: No direct return to the town, but chapter 14 sharpens the mental image into a fortification under repeated stress: walls still manned, wounded burning, and a city holding only provisionally.
sensoryProfile.sound: Relay language implies shouted orders, priests working under pressure, and a held line never quiet long enough to trust.
sensoryProfile.smell: Smoke, triage heat, and the implied reek of bodies burned before mourning can finish.
sensoryProfile.touch: Present only as carried urgency — a place so unstable the road feels hotter simply because its name enters the conversation.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: Chapter 14 reclassifies Hearthglen from crisis point to failing buffer. It is still standing, but only in the sense that a furnace has gates.

establishedFacts.fact: Hearthglen supports a large disciplined field force under Arthas with enough infrastructure to mobilize rapidly.
The camp is already treating sickness the Light does not easily answer, which means the plague has reached the force before the briefing finishes.
Its defenses and rhythms are configured for visible war even while the real threat is moving through grain and stores.
By chapter 13 Hearthglen is already overwhelmed, so it can no longer function as a stable fallback point or delayed briefing site.
Hearthglen held only provisionally and nearly failed again after dark after the first crisis wave.
Bodies at Hearthglen keep answering after short lulls, forcing the field into immediate burning before families are ready.
Uther remains on the field at Hearthglen even after Arthas drives the main body onward toward Stratholme.

eventsHere.event: Sylvanas forces access through the checkpoint and briefs Arthas on the plague as a grain-road war.
Arthas turns the briefing into immediate granary controls and mounted mobilization toward Hearthglen’s southern line.
Uther privately names Arthas’s flaw outside the pavilion while the column forms around them.
A south-track relay reports that Hearthglen has been overwhelmed and Arthas is already moving on Stratholme, collapsing the trio's available time to almost nothing.
Relay traffic reports that Hearthglen nearly fails again after dark, with the wounded burned early because the dead keep standing back up.
Arthas does not remain to consolidate Hearthglen; he takes the main body south-east toward Stratholme while Uther stays tied to the still-failing field.
